title: Walker DoS caps warn only through logs, not tool results
summary: Depth/node/.gitignore caps stop gracefully, but cap-hit signals are only console warnings and are not propagated in MCP/code-graph result metadata.

title: Save-time excluded-path rejection lacks a stable error code
summary: The invariant rejects before DB indexing, but generic Error rethrow prevents clients from keying on a stable index-scope rejection code.

title: Exported chunking helper has an unguarded fallback tier write
summary: The production memory_save caller passes prepared parsed data and guarded metadata application, but the exported helper's fallback and safe-swap update can write parsed.importanceTier directly if called internally.
Cleanup apply runs in one transaction and rebuilds plan inside transaction snapshot; cleanup audit insert is fail-closed inside that transaction.
Memory-save and code-graph specificFiles use realpath/canonical path for SSOT checks; symlink regression tests cover safe-looking paths resolving into z_future.
Walker DoS caps exist and warn, but warnings are not structured in operator-facing results.
Core mutation surfaces guard promotion/downgrade paths; exported chunking helper fallback remains a defense-in-depth gap.
Audit insert failures are caught by save/update/post/vector paths and preserve invariant outcome; cleanup intentionally fails closed.
Excluded paths reject before indexing writes, but rejection lacks a stable MCP error code.
No evidence that cleanup commits without downgrade audit rows; audit insert is inside the cleanup transaction and uncaught, so failure aborts the transaction.
No evidence that the tested symlink path safe/spec -> z_future bypasses memory-save or code-graph specificFiles; regression tests cover both and canonical path checks use realpath results.
Prior P1-001 has a security angle for poisoned checkpoint restore, but it is not re-emitted because the existing finding already owns the storage-boundary gap.
